pattern: DP over (index, finger1_pos, finger2_pos) states collapses to (index, other_finger_pos) because one finger is always on the just-typed letter, reducing complexity to O(n * 27).
hints: After typing position i, one finger must be on word[i]. Track only where the OTHER finger is — 26 letters or 'nowhere' (not yet placed).

From each state (i, other_pos) decide: move finger-on-word[i] to word[i+1], paying dist(word[i], word[i+1]); or move other-finger to word[i+1] for free (it becomes the new current finger) and the previous current-finger position becomes other_pos.
Use letter indices 0-25 for positions and 26 for 'unplaced'; the keyboard grid maps index c to row c//6, col c%6.

edge_inputs.description: two-letter word, each finger handles one letter at zero cost
all same letter — one finger stays put, zero movement
two letters that are neighbours on the keyboard
optimal assignment requires recognising when to switch fingers mid-word
repeated distant letters benefit from alternating fingers
letters at the four keyboard corners

stress.description: random uppercase word of length up to 200
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(2, size)
    return [''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_uppercase) for _ in range(n))]
